methodology_change: Replaced bi-encoder top-5 candidate pre-filtering with domain-aware exhaustive scoring. Each FDE routed to applicable WIDAI domains based on O*NET taxonomy analysis. Every KSA in applicable domains scored through full cross-encoder pipeline. 34,763 total pairs scored (vs v2's 629).

completeness_checks.note: All 126 O*NET FDEs evaluated. 14 identified as out-of-scope via domain routing (returned empty domain list). 112 FDEs routed to applicable domains. 108 FDEs produced at least one qualifying mapping. 18 FDEs classified as No Relationship. Every mapping row has a corresponding rationale file.

rationale_documentation.note: All 5422 scored rationale files contain STS primary + 4 secondary methods (BERTScore, NLI with softmax-corrected probabilities, Bi-Encoder, Jaccard). All 18 No Relationship files document best candidate STS score to justify classification.

scf_comparison.assessment: Higher Intersects With percentage is expected: exhaustive domain scoring surfaces all qualifying intersections, and O*NET general occupational competencies are broader than individual WIDAI KSAs, precluding Subset Of or Equal classifications.

relationship_type_consistency.no_relationship_validation: 18 FDEs (14.3%) classified as No Relationship. These include: Food Production, Building/Construction, Mechanical, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Geography, Medicine/Dentistry, Fine Arts, History/Archeology, Philosophy/Theology, Transportation, Time Management, Spatial Orientation, Selective Attention, Repairing Mechanical Equipment, Assisting/Caring for Others, Performing for the Public. All are genuinely outside WIDAI data/AI workforce scope.

strength_scoring.scoring_methodology: Cross-Encoder STS (stsb-roberta-base) as primary, raw 0-1 mapped to SCF-aligned discrete scale (3, 5, 8, 10). NLI probabilities via softmax.

scf_comparison.assessment: Lower strength-5 percentage reflects the exhaustive scoring approach — all qualifying intersections are captured, including many weak (strength 3) relationships that the bi-encoder pre-filter in v2 excluded. When filtered to strength >= 5, 1,751 mappings across 414 unique KSAs provide a rich, high-confidence mapping layer.
Much higher mappings per FDE reflects exhaustive domain scoring rather than pre-filtered top-N approach. For analysis purposes, strength >= 5 subset has mean 17.9 per FDE, median 8.0.

one_to_many_validation.domain_concentration_check: PASS — All 12 domains represented. Well-distributed. Max domain (AI, 12.1%) within acceptable range.

methodology_validation.candidate_generation: Domain-aware routing based on O*NET taxonomy analysis. No bi-encoder pre-filter. 14 FDEs identified as out-of-scope (returned empty domain list). 112 FDEs routed to applicable domains.
methodology_validation.scoring: Full multi-method on all domain-routed pairs (34,763 pairs scored in two massive batch predict() calls)
methodology_validation.rationale_type: Semantic (appropriate for O*NET knowledge/skill/ability descriptions mapped to WIDAI KSA statements)

gap_analysis.detection_method: FDEs with only strength-3 mappings where the O*NET element describes a competency within WIDAI scope. Out-of-scope FDEs validated against taxonomy.
gap_analysis.note: 6 of 7 v2 gap KSAs validated as resolved by new KSAs: Persuasion, Judgment/Decision Making, Computers/Electronics, Systems Analysis, Conflict Resolution, Updating Knowledge. All appear as top matches in their triggering FDEs. 1 genuine gap remains: Staffing (LS-T-011 exists but scores below threshold across all O*NET occupations).
gap_analysis.status: PASS — Gaps identified and documented. 6/7 v2 gaps validated as resolved by new KSAs.

v2_to_v3_comparison.improvement_summary: v3 produces 15.2x more mapping rows, surfaces 3.0x more unique KSAs (97.4% pool utilization vs 32.4%), eliminates bi-encoder pre-filter dependency. Lower mean STS reflects exhaustive capture of all qualifying relationships including weak intersections. Strength >= 5 subset (1,751 rows, 414 KSAs) provides high-confidence mapping layer. 6 of 7 v2 gap KSAs validated as top matches in their triggering FDEs.
